supporting_observations.observation: Stage 0x0E compares client_state +0x20 against 2 and chooses endpoint record +0x20 versus +0x30 from client_root +0x19970 before opening the transport.
State 0x50 uses the same client_state +0x20 field as its top-level branch condition before selecting the outgoing packet descriptor.
On the route-selector==2 branch, client_state +0x100 == 1 cmove-selects descriptor 0x140E59850 instead of 0x140E59810.
On the route-selector!=2 branch, the same client_state +0x100 == 1 check cmove-selects descriptor 0x140E59850 instead of 0x140E59818.

state_0x50_selection_matrix.bounded_inference: Opcode 0x1E overrides both base families rather than representing only the route-selector==2 branch.

notable_followup.observation: Calls 0x140154BC0 to inject the RSA-wrapped login/bootstrap block after writing the outer packet header fields.
The +0x100 == 1 override branch rejoins later logic through a dedicated short path rather than the ordinary opcode-0x10 body.
Also calls 0x140154BC0 after a near-identical outer-header prelude, showing that the RSA block wrapper is shared even though the outer opcode family differs.
Its +0x100 == 1 override path enters a different body than the route-selector==2 branch, so opcode 0x1E still rides two surrounding control-flow contexts.

corrected_conclusion: The earlier state-0x50 template picture was incomplete. The builder does not simply choose between opcode 0x10 and opcode 0x1E. Instead, client_state +0x20 first selects one of two base packet families that reuse the same route field as the stage-0x0E endpoint chooser: route-selector==2 uses descriptor 0x140E59810 (opcode 0x10), while route-selector!=2 uses descriptor 0x140E59818 (opcode 0x13). Then client_state +0x100 == 1 overrides either base family to descriptor 0x140E59850 (opcode 0x1E). This makes opcode 0x1E an override family layered on top of the route selection, not just the second half of a simple 0x10-versus-0x1E split.
impact_on_prior_artifacts: The earlier artifact that tied state 0x50 only to 0x140E59810 and 0x140E59850 remains correct for the route-selector==2 subpath but is incomplete as a global model.

The stage-0x0E connect/open artifact and the state-0x50 packet-family artifact now reinforce each other: both consume the same client_state +0x20 selector against the same client_root +0x19970 route table.
next_targets: Find the setters and semantic source of client_state +0x20 so the route-selector field can be named concretely rather than structurally.
Find the writers for client_state +0x100 to explain when the opcode-0x1E override activates.
Continue the normal-login identity-tail work by tracing real setters for client_state +0x140, +0x148, +0x130, and +0x138.
